chunks.text: The Computer Graphics Group studies scientific visualization, physically-based rendering, animation, computer-aided geometric design, rapid prototyping, computer aided cornea modeling and visualization, medical imaging, tele-immersive environments for geographically distributed collaboration, and virtual environments for surgical simulation.
chunks.question: What areas does the Computer Graphics Group study?

chunks.text: Computer Aided Design and Modeling: Computer aided geometric design. Splines and subdivision. Surface optimization. Texture synthesis. Sound synthesis. Rapid prototyping. Rendering: Image based rendering. Vision realistic rendering. Computational photography and video. Visualization: Scientific and information visualization. Virtual environments, tele-immersive environments Animation: Motion capture analysis and synthesis. Physically based simulation. Video games. Art: Abstract sculpture, art & mathematics
chunks.question: What topics are covered in graphics research?
chunks.answer: Design, rendering, visualization, animation, and art.

chunks.text: Research Centers: Berkeley Center for Computational Imaging, Berkeley Center for New Media, Berkeley Institute of Design, Center for Augmented Cognition, Computer Graphics at Berkeley, FHL Vive Center for Enhanced Reality, Tele-Immersion, Video and Image Processing Lab, Visual Computing Lab
chunks.question: Which research centers are associated with graphics?
chunks.answer: Nine centers including computational imaging and design.

chunks.question: Who are the primary and secondary faculty?
chunks.answer: Six primary and twelve secondary faculty members.

chunks.question: What awards have faculty received?
chunks.answer: ACM Prize, NAE, Academy, Citation, and Sloan awards.

chunks.text: Related Courses: CS 39J. Freshman/Sophomore Seminar, CS 184. Foundations of Computer Graphics, CS 274. Computational Geometry, CS C280. Computer Vision, CS 284A. Foundations of Computer Graphics
chunks.question: Which courses are related to graphics?
chunks.answer: Five courses including graphics and vision.
